rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical bromocyclization of N-aryl acrylamides with KBr to form bromo-substituted indolo[2,1-a]isoquinolines
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of bromide (Br-) from KBr to generate Br radical (Path A) or Br2/bromonium (Path B); cathodic reduction of protons to H2

key_intermediates.name: bromine radical (Br•) from anodic oxidation of Br-
C-centered radical E from Br• addition to olefin of 1a
cyclohexadienyl radical F after 6-endo-trig cyclization onto aryl ring
arenium cation G after further anodic oxidation (or Br2 oxidation) of F
bromonium ion H from Br2 addition to olefin (Path B)
Wheland/arenium intermediate I after electrophilic cyclization (Path B)

electron_flow: At the anode, Br- is oxidized to Br• (or further to Br2); in Path A, Br• adds to the acrylamide olefin to give C-radical E, which undergoes 6-endo-trig cyclization onto the pendant aryl to give radical F, then a second anodic oxidation (or Br2) yields arenium cation G, which loses H+ to afford 4a. Meanwhile, protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.
notes: Two pathways are proposed. Favored Path A is a radical cascade (Br• addition / 6-endo-trig / oxidation / deprotonation), analogous to the authors' sulfonyl-radical cyclization to indolo[2,1-a]isoquinolines. Alternative Path B is an electrophilic bromocyclization via a bromonium ion H and arenium I. Mechanism is proposed based on prior literature analogy rather than dedicated probe experiments shown on this page.
